question: What's included in base pricing? What costs extra? Can pricing increase during contract?
title: Pricing Transparency, Total Cost of Ownership, and Price Protection
bullets: Enumerate all items included in base pricing (features, usage quotas, support tiers, environments, data retention)
List all add-ons and overage models with clear units and formulas (e.g., per user, per request, per GB, per region)
Disclose all potential extra fees (implementation, migration, support tiers, API calls, storage, egress, premium features)
Define price protection terms (caps, notice periods, renewal terms) and provide example invoices/estimates
suggested_inputs: Pricing sheet with line items, units, and formulas
Sample invoice/bill for a representative customer scenario
Total cost calculator or estimator with assumptions
Contract terms covering price changes, caps, and notice periods

good.text: Base pricing includes a clearly enumerated set of features (core functionality, defined usage quotas, standard support). Add-ons are explicitly listed with unit pricing (e.g., per user/API call/GB) and no hidden fees.
Overages are defined with precise formulas and thresholds (e.g., $X per 1k requests beyond quota). A total cost calculator and sample invoices are provided for common scenarios.
Pricing increases are contractually capped (e.g., ≤X% annually) with Y months advance notice. Mid-term price changes are not allowed; adjustments apply only at renewal with agreed limits.
We disclose total cost of ownership, including implementation, migration, support tiers, data egress, and premium features. We provide a detailed example bill for a representative workload.

partial.text: We provide general pricing tiers, but detailed inclusions, add-ons, and unit pricing are not fully itemized.
Pricing depends on usage, but the exact metrics, thresholds, and formulas for overages are not clearly defined.
Additional fees may apply (e.g., support, storage), but a complete list and costs are not transparently documented.
Price changes are possible at renewal, but caps, notice periods, and mid-contract protections are not clearly specified.

bad.text: Pricing is not disclosed; customers must contact sales for details.
Pricing depends on usage without defining units, thresholds, or formulas.
Additional fees may apply, but they are not listed or quantified.
Prices are subject to change without caps or notice periods.

follow_ups: Provide an all-in cost for a specific scenario (users, requests, storage, regions) with a sample invoice.
Detail overage charges and exact formulas—what thresholds and rates apply?
List all fees not included in base price (implementation, support tiers, egress, premium features).
What contractual protections exist against mid-contract price increases and what caps apply at renewal?
Share a recent customer bill showing all charges and how they map to usage.
